title: crataegus monogyna subsp. intermedia
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. intermedia, a lesser-known variant of the Midland hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the rose family and native to Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiotonic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. The health benefits of Crataegus monogyna subsp. intermedia include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially alleviating symptoms of menopausal disorders. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support cardiovascular well-being and provide antioxidant protection.
